lyrics:
Tried to walk the straight-line way
Spoke in whispers, paid my dues
Watched the dreamers drift away
Held their shadows ’til they flew
All my good intentions fade
Like paint that’s been weathered
If virtue’s just a losing trade
Then fortune’s looking better

They say you gotta be good
Do the work, spend the time
I’ve been doin’ all I should
But I still can’t find a dime

If being good ain’t good enough
I’ll take my chance with luck-
’Cause being lucky is better…
Everything’s better than nothin’

Saw a preacher count his coins
While a beggar blessed the rain
Traders toast their crooked gains
And saints bear silent pain
I’ve been waitin’ for a sign
But the heavens just dismiss
If grace won’t cross that line
I’ll roll the dice and wish

They say you gotta be good
Do the work, spend the time
I’ve been doin’ all I should
But I still can’t find a dime

If being good ain’t good enough
I’ll take my chance with luck-
’Cause being lucky is better…
Everything’s better than nothin’

So here’s to all the ones who tried
And stumbled in the dust
We built our dreams, swallowed our pride
Still fate betrayed our trust
If goodness buys no favor, friend
Then I’ll just tempt the spin
Let the wheel decide the end
And see who cashes in

They say you gotta be good
Do the work, spend the time
I’ve been doin’ all I should
But I still can’t find a dime

If being good ain’t good enough
I’ll take my chance with luck-
’Cause being lucky is better…
Everything’s better than nothin’

They say you gotta be good
Do the work, spend the time
I’ve been doin’ all I should
But I still can’t find a dime

If being good ain’t good enough
I’ll take my chance with luck-
’Cause being lucky is better…
Everything’s better than nothin’

Everything’s better than nothin’
Everything’s better than nothin’
Everything’s better than nothin’
Everything’s better than nothin’
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track features a dense, gritty blues-rock texture, creating a defiant and swaggering emotional atmosphere. The energy is consistently high and driving, propelled by a tight rhythm section that gives the song an infectious shuffle feel. The production is polished yet powerful, with the fiery lead guitar and assertive male vocals sitting clearly atop a punchy bass and drum foundation. The track's identity is defined by the explosive guitar solo and the catchy, anthemic chorus, where powerful background vocals respond to the lead singer's hook.

review.review_text: PCBender plants a flag squarely in the tradition of working-man rock philosophy here, and the hook earns its keep: 'If being good ain't good enough / I'll take my chance with luck' lands with the kind of plain-spoken weight you get from John Hiatt or early Tom Petty when they stopped trying to be clever. The shuffle groove at 89 BPM in C minor keeps things driving without rushing, and the guitar solo hits like a genuine release rather than decoration. The anthemic chorus, stacked with background vocals pushing back against the lead, is the kind of thing that holds a room.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'If being good ain't good enough / I'll take my chance with luck'
